title: Removing Contiguous Duplicates from Rotated Array

statement.markdown:
Given a rotated array containing integers, remove all contiguous duplicates. The input array has at least one unique element.

Example:

Input: ["2 1 1 2 2 1 3"]

Output: ["1 2 3"]

Note: If the input contains only one unique element, return the same.

Input: ["1 1 1 1"]

Output: ["1"]
statement.notes: No specific rules other than the array has at least one unique element

examples.explanation: Remove contiguous duplicates from the input array
Only one unique element in the array

hints.text: Use two pointers to iterate over the array

editorial.title: Removing Contiguous Duplicates from Rotated Array
editorial.intuition: The two pointer approach can be used to remove contiguous duplicates by moving the second pointer only when the elements at the two pointers are different
editorial.bruteForce: Generate all possible combinations of unique elements from the rotated array
editorial.betterSolution: Use two pointers to iterate over the array and remove contiguous duplicates
editorial.optimalSolution: Two pointers approach, only increment the second pointer when the elements at the two pointers are different
